headline: Previously Rejected Articles Now Recovered
subject: Recovered Articles

article_items.title: pm-skills
article_items.description: 205 professional Agent Skills (PRDs, launch plans, postmortems, rubrics, contracts…) as importable building blocks for Python AI agents — LangChain, CrewAI, LlamaIndex, or any framework.
article_items.text: 205 professional Agent Skills (PRDs, launch plans, postmortems, rubrics, contracts…) as importable building blocks for Python AI agents — LangChain, CrewAI, LlamaIndex, or any framework. Tags agent-skills , ai-agents , crewai , langchain , llm , mcp , prompts 205 professional Agent Skills — PRDs, launch plans, postmortems, rubrics, contracts, pitch decks and more, across 21 professions — as importable building blocks for Python AI agents. The skills are bundled with the package, so there's no network call and no API key to read them. Core (no dependencies) Any other framework works too — skill_prompt(name, task) returns plain text you can drop into any LLM call. Same skills run live in the browser playground , over MCP , or via npx pm-claude-skills . Tags agent-skills , ai-agents , crewai , langchain , llm , mcp , prompts Release history Release notifications | RSS feed Copy a direct link to the current filters https://pypi.org/project/pm-skills/0.1.0/#files Copy The following attestation bundles were made for pm_skills-0.1.0.tar.gz : Publisher: publish-python.yml on mohitagw15856/pm-claude-skills Predicate type: https://docs.pypi.org/attestations/publish/v1 Subject digest: 7b69db566f2133fda92e7317a1df4904669c363a233c187025d2bf273ecfdf32 Publication workflow: publish-python.yml@940754f4fe1ccc6f358788a219fb579068bc6aba The following attestation bundles were made for pm_skills-0.1.0-py3-none-any.whl : Publisher: publish-python.yml on mohitagw15856/pm-claude-skills Predicate type: https://docs.pypi.org/attestations/publish/v1 Subject digest: 7330d38d62d894d17dc15d8f6b0bc1b7b1c67e23eb6a8ef0e7ab6f146c070b51 Publication workflow: publish-python.yml@940754f4fe1ccc6f358788a219fb579068bc6aba Contributing to PyPI Developed and maintained by the Python community, for the Python community.
